item.title: Oracle’s Credit Risk Measure Nears Record High on AI-Debt Fears
item.description: A gauge of Oracle Corp.’s credit risk is nearing an all-time closing high on Friday, the latest sign that investors are growing increasingly wary about the technology giant’s heavy debt load amid rising oil prices and falling stock prices.

item.title: Russia Set to Ban Gasoline Exports From April 1 as Prices Jump
item.description: Russia plans to ban gasoline exports for the nation’s producers from April 1 to meet domestic demand as global fuel prices surge amid the war in Iran.

item.title: Goldman Traders Warn Against Shorting Stocks Amid ‘Upside’ Risk
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s trading desk is warning investors not to turn bearish on US stocks, saying current positioning leaves the market vulnerable to a short squeeze if geopolitical tensions ease.

item.title: Total Inks Deal to Use EDF’s Nuclear Power for French Refineries
item.description: Oil major TotalEnergies SE, which is diversifying into renewable and gas-fired electricity output, struck a 12-year deal to buy Electricite de France SA’s nuclear power to feed its French refineries and chemical sites.

item.title: The Potential for Real Misery
item.description: The stagflation of the 1970s wasn’t just an oil shock.

item.title: European Commission’s Data Stolen in Hack on AWS Account
item.description: The European Commission was hit by a cyberattack that may have resulted in the theft of internal data, months after another incident potentially exposed some staff details.

item.title: EPA Finalizes Higher Biofuels Quota to Bolster Domestic Demand
item.description: The Trump administration unveiled long-awaited biofuels blending standards, giving a stronger mandate than a plan proposed last year in a further show of support for American farmers and biofuels producers.

item.title: It Sucks to Work in the Video-Game Industry Right Now
item.description: Even developers who successfully release big hits, like Fortnite and Battlefield, are losing their jobs

item.title: ECB Shouldn’t Rush Iran Response or Overreact, Schnabel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank shouldn’t rush its response to the Iran war and must be careful not to “overreact,” Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel said, while urging officials to remain agile and vigilant.

item.title: Paraguay’s Economy Grew 6.6% in 2025, Fastest Pace in 12 Years
item.description: The land-locked South American country posted its highest growth in more than a decade last year, with the economy expanding 6.6%.

item.title: First Brands to Sell 12 Brands Including Autolite for $25 Million
item.description: Bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands Group agreed to sell some of its most-recognizable brands for $25 million after losing rescue funding and major customers.

item.title: Brightline Florida Said to Work With Adviser on Troubled Railroad’s Debt Talks
item.description: Brightline Trains Florida LLC, the struggling private rail line connecting Orlando to Miami, is working with Perella Weinberg Partners on a potential debt restructuring and equity raise, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: India Acts to Curb Forex Speculation as Rupee Plumbs New Lows
item.description: India’s central bank stepped up its defense of the battered rupee, rolling out a measure to curb speculation after it slid to a new low amid concerns of a widening trade gap driven by the US-Iran war.

item.title: Surging Oil, Gold Prices Lift Toronto Stocks as S&P 500 Drops
item.description: Canadian stocks rose Friday on higher gold and oil prices, defying the swoon across major US and European indexes as traders price in the potential for a longer Iran war.

item.title: Chile Inflation Expectations Spike to Highest Since March 2023
item.description: Chile’s one-year breakevens — a measure of inflation expectations — jumped to the highest since March 2023 on Friday after the government loosened a fuel stabilization mechanism, triggering a jump in gasoline prices.

item.title: Biggest Euro-Zone Price Jump Since 2022 Seen in First G-20 Data
item.description: The euro zone suffered the biggest inflation jump this month since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022, according to economists assessing the fallout from war in the Middle East.

item.title: Asia’s Air Travel Crisis Risks Spreading on Iran War’s Jet Fuel Squeeze
item.description: The aviation crisis starting to grip Asia is threatening to intensify and spread to Europe and beyond, as energy turmoil caused by the Iran war collides with seasonal travel demand.

item.title: Sixth Street Nears Deal for 80% of Sunderland Women’s Team
item.description: Sixth Street Partners, one of the most active private equity investors in football, is nearing an acquisition of a majority stake in Sunderland AFC’s women’s team, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Pimco Leads Foreign Purchases of Colombia Debt Ahead of Vote
item.description: Overseas investors led by Pacific Investment Management Company are piling into Colombian local peso bonds ahead of presidential elections that may herald a radical change of government.

item.title: Shale Oil Drilling Growth Primed to Restart in 2026, Citi Says
item.description: Higher oil prices driven up by the conflict in the Middle East stand to push some of America’s biggest shale producers to start adding drilling rigs in the second half of this year and more than 100,000 barrels per day of increased output by 2027, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: ‘Mispriced’ Warner Bros. Deal Spread Creates Windfall Potential
item.description: Paramount Skydance Corp. may have won out against Netflix Inc. in the fight to buy Warner Bros. Discovery Inc., but there are enough market cross-currents and doubts around the pending $111 billion deal to make buying shares of the target an enticing wager.

item.title: Fed’s Barkin Says Inflation Progress May Be At Risk of Stalling
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Richmond President Tom Barkin said the US-Israel war in Iran threatens to add to already elevated inflationary pressures and clouds the economic outlook at a time when the labor market is fragile.

item.title: Cyber Stocks Sink on Report Anthropic AI Model Poses Security Risks
item.description: Cybersecurity stocks slumped on Friday after a Fortune report raised concern that an Anthropic PBC artificial intelligence model being tested may be used by hackers to skirt current cyber defenses.

item.title: Sony Raises PlayStation 5 Prices By Up to $150 Citing Economic Pressures
item.description: Sony Group Corp. raised the prices of its PlayStation 5 video-game console lineup by as much as $150 on Friday, attributing the hike to “continued pressures in the global economic landscape.”

item.title: CVC Is Said to Explore Divestments for Recordati After Takeover
item.description: CVC Capital Partners Plc plans to explore options including potential divestments for Recordati SpA if it succeeds in its €10.9 billion ($12.5 billion) takeover of the Italian drugmaker, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ambani’s Jio Mega IPO Is Said to Set Bank Fees in Line With NSE
item.description: Reliance Industries Ltd. has set investment banking advisory fees for the planned initial public offering of its telecom unit at about 0.65% of the issue size, according to people familiar with the matter, largely in line with those to be paid by National Stock Exchange of India Ltd.

item.title: Treasury Yields Reach Year’s Highs as Iran Stalemate Lifts Oil
item.description: Treasuries fell, lifting benchmark yields to the highest levels this year, as oil prices resumed the advance unleashed by the US war on Iran, which is entering its fifth week.

item.title: Why a Major Clean Energy Firm Chose Brookfield Over Staying Public
item.description: Also: BMO hires new equities leader from CIBC

item.title: Ethiopia Signs $13.1 Billion of Energy, Mining Investment Deals
item.description: Ethiopia has signed $13.1 billion of investment deals spanning renewable energy, manufacturing, real estate, mining and green ammonia, according to the government.

item.title: Carnival Cuts Profit Outlook as Iran War Pushes up Fuel Cost
item.description: Carnival Corp. cut its full-year profit outlook as surging crude prices are driving up fuel costs.
